_doc: The two diagnostic config entries the bootstrap creates. AT LEAST TWO entries required; AT LEAST ONE must be a subset (fewer than the full canonical pollutant set). bootstrap.py asserts this — see cleanroom/bootstrap.py:_assert_matrix_invariants. The subset entry (B) is the diagnostic that distinguishes 'migration preserved selection' from 'reset to default-all'. An all-pollutants-only test proves nothing.

entries.name: munich-all-pollutants
entries._role: common-case: all canonical pollutants. Validates the happy path.

entries.name: graz-subset-pm25-pm10
entries._role: DIAGNOSTIC: subset selection. THIS ENTRY IS NON-OPTIONAL. If migration silently resets to default-all, only Entry B catches it. Gate D asserts this entry's selection survives identical post-upgrade.
